LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PATRICIA LURIE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS FILED ON DECEMBER 5, 2011. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-12-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
REPLY IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S DEMURRER TO PLTF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT LURIE, PATRICIA AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PATRICIA LURIE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION AND WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH, NINTH, TENTH, ELEVENTH, TWELFTH, THIRTEENTH AND FOURTEENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS DEFICIENT BECAUSE IT DOES NOT ALLEGE VIOLATION OF A PUBLIC POLICY GROUNDED IN A STATE OR FEDERAL CONSTITUTIONAL PROVISION OR STATUTE. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THE LACK OF MERIT OF THE SEVENTH, NINTH, TENTH, ELEVENTH, TWELFTH, THIRTEENTH AND FOURTEENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LA VENA WARD, CSR # 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.DEFENDANT PATRICIA LURIE'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JULY 17, 2012. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-12-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-17-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PATRICIA LURIE DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE AND OVERRULED AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, SUFFICIENT FACTS TO DEMONSTRATE DEFENDANT RETAINED PLAINTIFF'S PERSONAL PROPERTY FOR A WRONGFUL USE OR WITH INTENT TO DEFRAUD. LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ADEQUATELY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Melinda S. Riechert, Esq. of Morgan Lewis, Tel. 650.843.7530, appearing for defendants and moving party;
Samantha R. Nilsen, Esq. of Kumin Sommers LLP, Tel. 415.434.4500, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PATRICIA LURIE DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for financial elder abuse and overruled as to the eighth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. As to the seventh cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if she can do so in good faith, sufficient facts to demonstrate Defendant retained Plaintiff's personal property for a wrongful use or with intent to defraud. Liberally construed, the eighth cause of action is adequately pled. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANT PATRICIA LURIE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the second cause of action and without leave to amend as to the seventh, ninth, tenth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action. The second cause of action is deficient because it does not allege violation of a public policy grounded in a state or federal constitutional provision or statute. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of the seventh, ninth, tenth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth and fourteenth causes of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: La Vena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (302/HEK)
